## Final Notes

faq.q: Does the mass of the pendulum affect the period?
faq.a: No, surprisingly. Only length and gravity matter. Galileo demonstrated this.

faq.q: How does amplitude affect the period?
faq.a: For small amplitudes (<15°), it doesn't. For larger angles, T increases about 1% per 10°.

faq.q: What's the period of a grandfather clock?
faq.a: With a 1-meter pendulum, T = 2 seconds. The pendulum counts seconds on each swing.

faq.q: What's the period of a pendulum on the Moon?
faq.a: Gravity is lower there, so T is greater. A 1-meter pendulum on the Moon has T = 4.94 seconds.

faq.q: Can I use this to measure gravity?
faq.a: Yes. By measuring T and L, you can calculate g. This was a pre-GPS historical method.
